name: Frontiers Transportation Svc
fleet_size_est: unknown (small fleet, transports 'Homeless and Highly Mobile' students)
districts_or_customers: claims 'more than two dozen' MN schools
recent_news: 2025: MPS approved a new contract (cap $2.7M through 2025-26) despite prior reporting (AlphaNews, 2025) that Frontier had been paid >$7M against a $3.6M contract cap; MPS finance chief placed on leave amid related investigation.
fit_notes: AVOID/HIGH RISK. Independent MN operator but actively entangled in MPS billing/over-invoicing controversy and related public investigation. Reputational, regulatory, and contract-stability risk make this an unsuitable tuck-in despite Summit-state geography.

name: Barnesville Bus Co
founded_year: FMCSA authority since March 2001 (company likely older)
recent_news: Featured in regional reporting (Dickinson Press) on small-district driver shortages; co-owner Debbie Jerger described long-tenured driver roster. No M&A activity identified.
website: https://www.barnesvillemn.com/ (city directory listing); company has no dedicated site identified
fit_notes: Genuine small family-owned MN school bus operator in Summit footprint, single-district concentration (Barnesville ISD). Very small scale (likely sub-20 buses) limits standalone value, but clean independent profile and long-tenured family ownership signal possible succession opportunity for a low-cost tuck-in.

name: Green Truck & Bus Solutions
owner_or_parent: n/a - not a school bus operator
recent_news: The website greentruckonthego.com is an organic food-truck/catering business (LA/San Diego/Houston), not a school-bus operator. No school-bus operator by this name confirmed at 4637 Market St, San Diego. Likely data error or defunct/misclassified entity.
fit_notes: NOT A TUCK-IN TARGET. Webdomain resolves to an organic food truck/catering company, not a student-transport operator. Either misclassified in source data or a dormant/non-operating entity; remove from pipeline.

name: Rudco Inc
owner_or_parent: formerly independent (Rick Sullivan family); current status unclear
founded_year: ~1984 (Sullivan ran it for 33 years until 2017 death)
fleet_size_est: small (single-tier; per source ~5 employees and ~$236K revenue suggests minimal active fleet)
districts_or_customers: Historically Passaic County NJ school districts; listed as NJ DOE school bus contractor in 2020-21 filings
recent_news: Owner/operator Rick Sullivan died Dec 18, 2017; no public successor or sale identified. Listed on NJ DOE 2020-21 contractor list, but post-2021 active status uncertain. Note: rudco.com domain belongs to an unrelated waste-equipment manufacturer (Rudco Products), NOT the Passaic school bus contractor.
website: website not confirmed - rudco.com is unrelated waste-equipment company; school-bus Rudco has no apparent dedicated site
fit_notes: Likely sub-scale or wind-down: founder died 2017 with no identified succession plan; revenue/employee profile suggests minimal active operations. Possibly acquirable as a license/customer-list tuck-in for an NJ operator, but small fleet and unclear current ownership/operating status make economics marginal. Verify current operating status before pursuing.

name: Centerline Charter Corp
fleet_size_est: medium (~50-100+ buses; D&B lists 100-249 employees and $10-20M revenue)
recent_news: Actively recruiting drivers (route pay ~$24-25/hr plus incentives); no M&A activity identified. Continues to operate as family-owned Twin Cities school-bus and charter provider.
website: centerlinecharter.com (URL returns 404 on direct fetch; LinkedIn/Facebook pages confirm active operations)
fit_notes: STRONG FIT. Genuine family-owned Twin Cities school bus + charter operator in Summit's MN footprint, with meaningful scale ($10-20M revenue, 100-249 employees) and anchor contracts in Saint Paul/Roseville districts. Founder-led (Leroy Rossow) with no apparent strategic owner = clean independent tuck-in candidate with credible succession angle.
